title: Re-integrate CallRail with Lead Docket
description:
Domain: hauptman-obrien.net
Summary: The client has reported that their CallRail account was deactivated and removed from Lead Docket, resulting in the deletion of all recorded calls and historical data. The client is requesting that CallRail be re-integrated with Lead Docket using the integration details available within Lead Docket. The client has also provided a support article from Lead Docket as a reference.
Client Goal: Restore the CallRail and Lead Docket integration so that call tracking data flows correctly into Lead Docket going forward.
Requirements for Completion:
  • CallRail is successfully re-integrated with Lead Docket
  • Integration is configured using the integration details available within Lead Docket
  • Call data from CallRail populates correctly in Lead Docket following re-integration
  • Integration is tested and confirmed functional before closing the ticket
Assets and References:
  • Lead Docket CallRail integration guide: https://support.leaddocket.com/hc/en-us/articles/360045475872-Integrating-CallRail

title: Ride Home update
description:
Hi this is similar to the other Safe ride home tasks we had.
 https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/safe-ride-home/ 

Hauptman O'Brien has their safe ride home campaign and needs updates made to the safe ride home page. Predrag may be best to tag in for this, as he's helped with the previous safe ride home campaign. 

Deadline: changes live March 11th, changes taken down March 18th
 
Direct from client:
As you know, we will be offering our Safe Ride Home? program for St. Patrick’s Day weekend, and we will need some temporary updates to the Safe Ride Home? page on the website. Attached, you’ll find the temporary banner we would like to use.
 
The campaign will go live on Saturday, March 14th at Noon and expire on Wednesday, March 18th at 12:00 AM, with vouchers worth $35. If we can aim to have this page live by Wednesday, March 11th, that would be ideal. Although the campaign doesn’t officially start until Saturday, this will give the community a chance to start claiming vouchers early and allow us to resolve any potential issues before it goes live. The page can return to default setting on Wednesday, March 18th.
 
1. Update banner image


2. Update yellow banner portion on SRH page:
  • Current: No current Safe Ride Home® events. Please check this page periodically as we are always adding new events.
  • Temporarily change to: pending still waiting for AM response, I'll add the info when available.
    Update please use this: Celebrate safely! Grab your Safe Ride Home® Uber voucher today—ready to use March 14th through 18th!
3. Update Uber info:
  • Uber Voucher Link: https://r.uber.com/RJZXURPGXTD   
  • Voucher Redemption Guide Link: https://www.uber.com/us/en/business/products/vouchers/how-to-use/ 
Client info:
Just as we’ve done in the past, we’ll need to add the form back to the Safe Ride Home? page. This form will require visitors to input their name, phone number, and email address. After submitting, the voucher will be sent to their email. I’ve also attached the email format they will receive with the Uber voucher link attached (https://r.uber.com/RJZXURPGXTD).

Bio:
A native of northeast Nebraska, Daniel Hartnett was born in Sioux City, Iowa in 1956 and raised on his family’s farm near Hubbard, Nebraska. He earned his undergraduate degree in 1978 and his Juris Doctor in 1981 from the University of Nebraska–Lincoln. Dan was admitted to the Nebraska and Iowa state bars in 1981 and later to the South Dakota bar, and is admitted to practice before all state and federal courts in Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota. Prior to joining Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, Dan practiced law for 42 years with Crary, Huff, Ringenberg, Hartnett & Storm, PC in Sioux City, Iowa, where his practice focused exclusively on civil litigation.

title: Add new Attorney: Kathryn Hartnett
description:
Kathryn Hartnett

**Please add her to the website, right after Kyle McGinn on the Attorneys page. She goes by "Kate", but her listing and header should be under Kathryn Hartnett.

Bio:
Kate is a proud Nebraskan and was raised in South Sioux City, Nebraska. She grew up running around the halls of her father’s law firm. From a young age, Kate knew that she would be an attorney.
 
Kate graduated from the University of Iowa in 2007, earning a Bachelor of Arts degree. She then earned her Juris Doctor from Creighton University School of Law in 2010.  
 
Kate has been practicing law in Iowa and Nebraska at the state and federal level since 2010. Prior to 2016, Kate handled personal injury, premises liability, title, and catastrophic construction defect claims.
 
As an attorney at Hauptman O’Brien, Kate specializes in workers’ compensation and personal injury claims across Iowa and Nebraska. Kate has given in-depth presentations on workers’ compensation law at the Nebraska Workers’ Compensation Symposium and the National Association of Subrogation Professionals annual meeting.
 
When she is not at the office, Kate enjoys swimming laps, reading, cooking, honing her trivia skills, being a founding member of the Omaha Food Club, and spending time with her husband and two sons.
 
 
Kate in the Community:
 
Kate is a passionate advocate for social justice and political activism. With a keen interest in politics, she actively seeks to contribute to causes that support what is right and just. Kate also finds joy in organizing and contributing to various food and toy drives, aiming to help those in need and spread joy to those less fortunate. She is deeply committed to setting a good example for her two sons, demonstrating the importance of giving back and standing up for what is right.

Iowa

Old:

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
 "@context": "https://schema.org",
 "@type": "WebSite",
 "url": "https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/",
 "name": "Hauptman, O'Brien, Wolf & Lathrop Personal Injury Lawyers",
 "alternateName": "Nebraska Personal Injury Lawyers Hauptman, O'Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, P.C., LLO",

{

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1: Add to the South Omaha GBP
https://maps.app.goo.gl/vD4G5j16R4E269Af8

Dash cams can be powerful evidence after a car accident, but they don’t always help your injury claim. While footage may clearly show another driver running a red light, making an unsafe lane change, or causing a crash, it can also reveal details that insurers use to shift blame. Video might capture speeding, delayed braking, or comments made in the car that are later taken out of context.

That’s why dash cam footage should always be preserved carefully and reviewed before sharing it with insurance companies. In Nebraska and Iowa injury claims, even small details can affect how fault is assigned and how much compensation you receive. If your dash cam recorded a crash, speaking with an experienced personal injury attorney can help ensure that evidence strengthens, rather than harms, your case.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-dash-cam-footage-can-help-or-hurt-your-injury-claim/



==================

POST #2: Add to the Omaha GBP.
https://maps.app.goo.gl/VbTBgQRWeEUm1Exz5

Admitting fault after a car accident can seriously affect your injury claim. In the stressful moments after a crash, many drivers say things like “I’m sorry” or “I didn’t see you,” without realizing those statements may later be used by insurance companies as admissions of liability. In Nebraska,  comparative fault laws mean your compensation can be reduced if you share responsibility, and if you’re found 50% or more at fault, you may not recover damages at all.

That’s why it’s important to avoid discussing blame at the scene. Instead, focus on safety, call law enforcement, document the accident, and exchange information. A personal injury attorney can review the evidence, challenge unfair fault claims, and help protect your right to fair compensation after a crash.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-badly-does-admitting-fault-affect-your-car-accident-claim/

Note: This blog post will be live on March 18, 2026.


=====================

POST #3: Add to the Bellevue GBP.
https://maps.app.goo.gl/Gbn1H6WxufimJigM8

If the other driver changes their story after a car accident, it can make your injury claim much more complicated. Some drivers initially admit fault at the scene but later give a different version of events to their insurance company. When statements conflict, insurers rely heavily on evidence to determine what actually happened. Photos of the scene, witness statements, police reports, traffic camera footage, and medical records can all play a key role in proving liability.

That is why it is important to document the accident carefully and seek medical attention as soon as possible. Strong evidence and consistent statements can help protect your credibility and prevent unfair fault assignments. An experienced car accident attorney can investigate the crash and help ensure the facts are accurately presented.

Learn More:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/102SQIMcPI7BLdGF1HuHhuPNynjKWeraCqK00tcsOcm8/edit?usp=sharing

Note: This blog post will be live on March 21, 2026.



=======================

POST #4: Add to the Lincoln GBP.
https://maps.app.goo.gl/tYeiHEGEC9VGLJGY9

After a car accident, many people wonder how much their settlement might be worth. The value of a claim depends on several factors, including the severity of injuries, medical expenses, lost income, property damage, and pain and suffering. Nebraska’s comparative negligence law can also affect compensation, since your recovery may be reduced if you share part of the fault.

Strong evidence such as police reports, medical records, photos, and witness statements can play a major role in determining settlement value. Insurance companies often try to minimize payouts, especially early in the process. Speaking with an experienced Lincoln car accident lawyer can help you understand the true value of your claim and ensure that any settlement reflects the full impact of the accident on your life.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-much-is-a-car-accident-settlement-worth-in-lincoln-ne/

Note: This blog post will be live on March 28, 2026.


=====================

POST #5: Add to the Council Bluffs GBP.
https://maps.app.goo.gl/tBWVj7cXMFjerL6B8

If you were injured in an accident in Iowa, you may be able to seek compensation for pain and suffering. These damages cover the physical pain and emotional impact an injury can cause, such as chronic discomfort, anxiety, loss of enjoyment of daily activities, or long-term limitations. Unlike medical bills or lost wages, pain and suffering damages do not have a fixed dollar value. Instead, they are evaluated based on factors like the severity of the injury, the length of recovery, and how the injury affects your daily life.

Iowa law may allow injured victims to recover these damages when another party’s negligence caused the accident. An experienced Iowa personal injury attorney can help gather evidence and demonstrate the full impact of the injury when pursuing fair compensation.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/can-you-sue-for-pain-and-suffering-in-iowa/

Note: This blog post will go live on March 29, 2026.

title: Problem With the Side Review Widget
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Predrag
description:
Hi, guys.

I found a potential problem with the side review widget that appears on most (if not all) pages, posts, blog paginations, and category paginations.

The widget appears on the right side of each page, typically under all the other elements there. It's supposed to look like this:


However, sometimes this widget pulls in the whole content of the article instead of a review: 


These screenshots were taken from the same blog post, opened in two separate browser windows:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-surveillance-footage-can-impact-your-slip-and-fall-case/

I don't know what causes it. I have tried to recreate this error, but nothing is consistent.

I have tried:
- opening pages/posts from the site's menu navigation
- opening/pages posts from other internal links
- copy/pasting the links in the browser
- launching pages/posts from Google searches
- launching pages/posts from Google sheets or docs
- launching pages/posts from other external websites.

It appears to occur randomly. Most of the time it doesn't occur, but sometimes it does. It is also corrected if the page is refreshed. After a page refresh, the widget appears correctly once more, pulling a review. 

Also, Screaming Frog has pointed out 80%+ duplication between some articles and the blog page where they appear as 1st in the category, such as these: 

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/factors-to-consider-before-settling-a-personal-injury-claim/
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/category/uncategorized/

The two pages above don't have duplicate content, but at some point, when I opened the /uncategorized/ page, the widget pulled the whole content of the first blog that appeared in it, causing content duplication.

I started seeing this around 2-3 weeks ago.
Also, I am using Google Chrome, Windows 11, on an Asus Vivobook laptop (if this matters).  

Please investigate and fix, if possible.

title: Pagination Reduction for /blog/  and /category/blog/
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Predrag
description:
Hi, guys. 

Is it possible to increase the number of posts on each blog page, and category blog page, to 15 per page while reducing the size of the featured images and keeping the character count for the text excerpts at around 125-130?

Should look something like this:
https://snyderac.com/about-us/blog/

The https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/ website currently has 227 /blog/ + /category/blog/, which is quite a bit. Full list below: 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1AcRrqtA63NILUqZmZWNhlNLLaTq2kjPJEpuB2Bo0syY/edit?usp=sharing

So, if this is possible without major site layout changes, please do so. 

This should help reduce crawl depth, improve crawl efficiency, and reduce the near-duplicate effect between the category pages.  

Thank you.

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1: Add to the Omaha, NE GBP.

https://maps.app.goo.gl/uD6CLqCjkfG9jvEJ9

After a car accident, exchanging contact and insurance information is more than a formality—it’s a legal requirement in Nebraska and Iowa. Once everyone is safe, drivers should share full names, addresses, phone numbers, driver’s license details, insurance company names, policy numbers, and vehicle information. Accuracy is essential, and taking photos of documents (with permission) can help prevent errors.

It’s equally important to stay calm and avoid discussing fault, as roadside statements can affect future claims. Documenting the exchange, gathering witness contact information, and reviewing the police report later can strengthen your position. Proper information exchange lays the groundwork for a smoother insurance and legal process while protecting your rights.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-to-document-vehicle-position-after-an-accident/


======================

POST #2: Add to the South Omaha, NE GBP.

https://maps.app.goo.gl/6mkDk3UKnX7iydnR7

After a car accident, exchanging contact and insurance information is a key step that can protect your rights and support a smoother claims process. In Nebraska and Iowa, drivers are legally required to stop and provide identifying details when a crash involves injury, death, or property damage.

Once it’s safe, exchange full names, addresses, phone numbers, driver’s license information, insurance company and policy details, and vehicle information. It’s also helpful to confirm accuracy using official documents and, when possible, take photos of insurance cards and licenses.

Keep the interaction calm and factual, and avoid discussing fault at the scene. Documenting the exchange and gathering witness contact information can also strengthen your records later.

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-to-properly-exchange-contact-and-insurance-information-after-a-car-accident/


======================

POST #3: Add to the Bellevue, NE GBP.

https://maps.app.goo.gl/S2aRofmA2QaJ1V7FA

Witness statements can play a major role in car accident claims, especially when fault is disputed. In Nebraska and Iowa, third-party accounts often help insurers and attorneys confirm key details like right of way, speed, or driver behavior before impact.

If the scene is safe and you’re able, approach witnesses calmly and ask if they’re willing to share what they saw. Collect basic contact information, and write down their observations as soon as possible while details are fresh. Focus on what they observed, not conclusions about who caused the crash.

Avoid leading questions, arguing, or pressuring anyone to give a formal statement on the spot. When handled correctly, witness information can strengthen your credibility and support a clearer, more efficient claims process.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/dos-and-donts-of-gathering-witness-statements-after-a-car-accident/


======================

POST #4: Add to the Lincoln, NE GBP
https://maps.app.goo.gl/SzbZfHYFX8b4bXZY9

A car accident can leave you shaken and unsure what to do next. The steps you take immediately after a crash can affect your health, your insurance claim, and your financial recovery under Nebraska’s modified comparative negligence rules. Prioritizing safety, documenting the scene, and seeking prompt medical care are critical.

  • After a car accident in Lincoln, NE, remember to:
  • Call 911 and request police and medical assistance
  • Accept medical evaluation, even if injuries seem minor
  • Take photos of vehicles, road conditions, and visible injuries
  • Exchange contact and insurance information
  • Seek follow-up medical care and keep records
  • Be cautious when speaking with insurance adjusters
  • Preserve dash cam footage or other evidence

Careful action early on can help protect your rights and strengthen your claim.

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/what-to-do-after-a-car-accident-in-lincoln-ne/


======================

POST #5: Add to the Council Bluffs GBP.
https://maps.app.goo.gl/hXU8HUcYFJvDdw9v5

After a car accident, business or traffic camera footage can play a critical role in an injury claim. Video may capture who entered an intersection first, whether a driver was speeding, or how the collision unfolded. Under Iowa’s comparative fault rules, your compensation can be reduced if you share responsibility for the crash. Clear footage can help establish fault and prevent insurers from unfairly shifting blame. However, many security systems automatically delete recordings within days, and some traffic cameras only provide live feeds without saving video.

Acting quickly is essential. Identifying nearby businesses, confirming camera locations, and requesting that footage be preserved can prevent valuable evidence from being lost. Early action can significantly affect how your claim is evaluated and resolved.

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1: Add to the main Omaha, NE GBP

1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States


When a workers’ compensation claim is delayed, the impact can be immediate and overwhelming. Medical bills don’t wait, and missed wage benefits can quickly create financial stress. Unfortunately, delays are often the result of insurance company tactics like repeated document requests, poor communication, or unnecessary reviews. These setbacks can affect your ability to get treatment approved and receive the benefits you’re owed.

Knowing the warning signs of a stalled claim and taking early action is critical. Clear documentation, prompt follow-up, and legal guidance can help stop delays before they cause long-term harm. Experienced workers' comp attorneys can step in to communicate with insurers, enforce deadlines, and pursue penalties when delays are unreasonable. You deserve a fair process and timely benefits while you focus on recovery.

#WorkersComp
#InjuredAtWork
#LegalHelp
#HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/what-happens-when-workers-comp-claims-are-delayed/


-------------------------------------------

POST #2: Add to the South Omaha, NE GBP

4939 S 24th St, Omaha, NE 68107, United States

Pedestrian accidents can leave victims dealing with serious injuries, medical bills, and uncertainty about how to recover compensation. One of the most important factors in a successful claim is strong evidence. Photos and videos from the scene, police reports, witness statements, and detailed medical records all help show how the crash happened and who was at fault. This information can prevent insurers from shifting blame or minimizing injuries.

Because evidence can disappear quickly, early action matters. Preserving photos, securing witness contact information, and documenting medical treatment can make a significant difference in the outcome of a claim. A skilled pedestrian accident lawyer knows how to gather, protect, and present this evidence effectively, especially when fault is disputed. With the right support, injured pedestrians can focus on healing while their legal rights are protected.

#PedestrianAccident
#PersonalInjury
#AccidentClaims
#HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/what-evidence-matters-most-in-a-pedestrian-accident-claim/


-------------------------------------------

POST #3: Add to the Council Bluffs, IA GBP.

607 S Main St, Council Bluffs, IA 51503, United States

Iowa reached a historic milestone in 2025 by recording its lowest number of traffic fatalities, reflecting years of safety improvements, stronger laws, and increased awareness. While this progress is encouraging, serious crashes and life-changing injuries still occur. Improved vehicle technology and faster emergency response mean more people survive accidents, but often with long-term medical needs and financial challenges.

As roads become safer overall, insurance companies may argue that severe injuries are less likely or try to minimize claims. That makes accountability more important than ever when negligence is involved. Our experienced car accident attorneys play a key role in protecting injured victims, proving fault, and pursuing full compensation. Safer roads are a success, but when a crash disrupts your life, your legal rights still matter and deserve strong advocacy.

#CarAccident
#PersonalInjuryLaw
#TrafficSafety
#HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/iowa-records-historic-low-traffic-deaths-in-2025/


-------------------------------------------

POST #4: Add to the Bellevue, NE GBP.

11507 S 42nd St # 101, Bellevue, NE 68123, United States

Failure to yield is one of the leading causes of serious crashes in Nebraska, often occurring at intersections, crosswalks, and during left turns or merges. These accidents may seem straightforward, but determining fault can quickly become complicated. Nebraska’s comparative negligence laws allow insurance companies to argue shared responsibility, which can reduce or even block compensation if the case isn’t handled carefully.

When a driver ignores right-of-way rules, the resulting injuries can be severe, especially for pedestrians, cyclists, and motorcyclists. Medical bills, lost income, and long-term recovery deserve proper attention, not rushed conclusions or low settlement offers. Experienced car accident lawyers know how to investigate failure-to-yield crashes, analyze traffic laws, and push back against insurer tactics. If negligence caused your injuries, strong legal guidance can make all the difference in protecting your rights and future.

#NebraskaLaw
#CarAccident
#InjuryClaim
#HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/nebraska-failure-to-yield-laws-what-drivers-must-know/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1: Add to the Omaha GBP

1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States

Modern vehicles record critical information in the seconds before and during a crash through event data recorders, often called a car’s “black box.” This data can show speed, braking, seat belt use, and other details that are frequently disputed after serious accidents in Nebraska and Iowa. When fault is unclear, crash data can help confirm what really happened and challenge inaccurate statements from other drivers or insurance companies.

Because this information can be overwritten or lost if a vehicle is repaired or destroyed, preserving it quickly is essential. Accessing and interpreting EDR data also requires technical knowledge and legal experience. When used correctly, it can strengthen injury claims, support fair settlements, and present clear evidence if a case goes to trial. Understanding how crash data works can make a meaningful difference in protecting your rights after an accident.

#CarAccidentLaw #PersonalInjuryClaims #CrashDataRecorder #KnowYourRights

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/crash-data-recorders-in-cars-how-they-influence-accident-cases/


============================

POST #2: Add to the South Omaha GBP

4939 S 24th St, Omaha, NE 68107, United States

Truck accidents often involve more than a single mistake. One critical factor is whether the truck driver and trucking company followed federal Hours of Service rules designed to prevent fatigue. These regulations limit how long drivers can be on the road and require adequate rest, because exhaustion slows reaction time and impairs judgment.

When Hours of Service violations occur, they can strongly support a truck accident claim. Electronic logs, GPS data, fuel receipts, and delivery records may reveal that a driver was pushed beyond legal limits or that a company ignored unsafe practices. This evidence can help show negligence, link fatigue directly to the crash, and even expose broader safety failures within a trucking operation.

Because this data can be lost or overwritten, acting quickly after a crash is essential. Understanding how Hours of Service violations affect liability can make a meaningful difference in holding the right parties accountable and pursuing full compensation.

#TruckAccidentLaw #HoursOfService #TruckDriverFatigue #PersonalInjuryClaims

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-hours-of-service-violations-can-strengthen-your-truck-accident-claim/


============================

POST #3: Add to the Council Bluffs GBP

607 S Main St, Council Bluffs, IA 51503, United States

Pedestrians in Iowa have important legal protections, but many people don’t realize how strong those rights are until an accident happens. Iowa law requires drivers to yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks, use extra caution in shared spaces, and remain alert in pedestrian-heavy areas. Even when jaywalking is involved, an injured pedestrian may still have the right to pursue compensation under Iowa’s comparative fault rules.

Pedestrian accidents often result in serious injuries, medical bills, lost income, and long-term challenges. Insurance companies may try to shift blame or minimize the impact of your injuries, which is why understanding your rights matters. An experienced pedestrian accident attorney can help gather evidence, challenge unfair fault claims, and fight for compensation that reflects the full scope of your losses.

If you or a loved one was injured while walking, legal guidance can make a meaningful difference in your recovery and peace of mind.

#IowaPedestrianLaw #PedestrianSafety #PersonalInjuryLaw #KnowYourRights

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/key-legal-rights-pedestrians-need-to-know-about-in-iowa/


=====================

POST #4: Add to the Bellevue GPB

11507 S 42nd St # 101, Bellevue, NE 68123, United States

When you’re injured in an accident, proving fault involves more than showing what happened. Courts look closely at causation, specifically actual cause and proximate cause. Actual cause focuses on what directly triggered the accident, using the “but for” test. Proximate cause goes a step further, asking whether the injuries were a foreseeable result of the negligent behavior.

This distinction plays a major role in personal injury cases across Iowa and Nebraska. Insurance companies often challenge causation to limit or deny compensation, especially when multiple events or parties are involved. Strong evidence, careful analysis, and experienced legal guidance are essential to connect the facts and establish fair responsibility.

Understanding how courts apply these principles can make a real difference in the outcome of a claim. An attorney who knows how to build both factual and legal causation arguments can help protect your rights and pursue the compensation you deserve.

#PersonalInjuryLaw #HauptmanOBrienAttorneys #AccidentClaims #LegalCausation #InjuryRights

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-courts-distinguish-between-actual-proximate-cause/

Note: This blog post is scheduled to go live on 12/18/2025.

POST #01: Add to the Bellevue GBP

11507 S 42nd St # 101, Bellevue, NE 68123, United States


After an accident, it can be easy to lose track of time while focusing on recovery. However, Nebraska law limits how long you have to file a personal injury claim. In most cases, you have four years from the date of the accident, but some situations have much shorter deadlines. Missing that window could mean losing your right to compensation completely.

At Hauptman O’Brien, our experienced Bellevue personal injury lawyers help clients understand filing deadlines, gather evidence, and take timely legal action. We work to protect your rights and ensure that no detail or deadline stands in the way of your recovery. If you were injured in Bellevue or anywhere in Nebraska, contact us today for a free consultation and let us help you move forward with confidence.

#PersonalInjuryLawyer #BellevueNE #AccidentAttorney #HauptmanOBrien

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-long-do-you-have-to-file-a-personal-injury-claim-in-bellevue-ne/



=============================


POST #02: Add to the main Omaha, NE GBP

1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States

After a crash, proving distracted driving can be challenging, but digital evidence can make all the difference. At Hauptman O’Brien, our attorneys know how to use cell phone records, GPS data, and app activity to uncover what really happened in the moments before impact.  This information can confirm whether a driver was texting, calling, or scrolling when they should have been watching the road. By combining this data with crash reconstruction, we build strong cases that insurance companies and juries can’t ignore.

If you were injured in an accident and suspect phone use was to blame, don’t wait; digital evidence can disappear fast. Contact our Nebraska and Iowa car accident lawyers today for a free consultation and protect your right to full compensation.

#DistractedDriving #CarAccidentLawyer #NebraskaLaw #HauptmanOBrien

Learn More:

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-cell-phone-records-can-prove-distracted-driving/

When you pay for insurance, you expect fair treatment and honest help when you need it most. Unfortunately, some insurance companies delay, underpay, or deny valid claims without justification. Under Iowa law, this may qualify as insurance bad faith. At Hauptman O’Brien, our Council Bluffs attorneys help clients hold insurers accountable when they break their duty to act in good faith.

We investigate claim denials, uncover internal communication records, and build strong cases to recover the compensation you deserve. Victims of bad faith may be entitled to damages for emotional distress, financial losses, and even punitive awards in serious cases. If you believe your insurer acted unfairly, don’t wait to get legal advice. Contact our Iowa personal injury lawyers today for a free consultation and protect your rights.

#InsuranceBadFaith #IowaLaw #CouncilBluffsAttorney #HauptmanOBrien

--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1: Add to the main Omaha, NE GBP

1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States

Determining who is liable for a truck accident isn’t always straightforward. While the truck driver or trucking company may be responsible, liability can also extend to the shipper or broker. A shipping company could be held accountable if it failed to properly package cargo, verify a carrier’s qualifications, or imposed unrealistic delivery deadlines. Similarly, brokers may face liability for hiring unsafe carriers or failing to comply with federal regulations.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, our experienced Omaha truck accident attorneys investigate every aspect of the crash to identify all negligent parties and pursue the compensation you deserve. We’re here to protect your rights and hold those responsible accountable.

#TruckAccidentLawyer #OmahaPersonalInjury #NebraskaLawFirm #HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/shipping-company-truck-accident-liability/


----------------------------------

POST #2: Add to the South Omaha, NE GBP

4939 S 24th St, Omaha, NE 68107, United States

Determining who’s responsible after a multi-vehicle collision can be complex. Investigators must piece together how the crash happened by examining police reports, witness statements, photos, and accident reconstruction data. Liability often depends on which driver’s actions, such as speeding, following too closely, or running a red light, set off the chain reaction. Sometimes, more than one party may share fault, including other drivers, commercial vehicle operators, or even those responsible for poor road maintenance.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, our experienced Omaha attorneys carefully analyze every detail to identify all liable parties and pursue maximum compensation for your injuries and losses. If you’ve been involved in a multi-car accident, don’t face the aftermath alone, our team is here to protect your rights and fight for justice.

#OmahaCarAccidentLawyer #MultiVehicleAccident #PersonalInjuryLaw #HauptmanOBrien

Learn more:

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-is-fault-determined-in-a-multiple-car-accident/


----------------------------------

POST #03: Add to the Bellevue, GBP

11507 S 42nd St # 101, Bellevue, NE 68123, United States

Truck accidents are different from ordinary car crashes, both in scale and complexity. If you were injured in a collision with a commercial truck in Bellevue, you need a law firm that understands the unique challenges these cases bring. At Hauptman O’Brien, our attorneys know how to uncover vital evidence such as electronic control module data, driver logs, and maintenance records to prove fault and strengthen your claim.

We move fast to preserve evidence, counter aggressive insurance tactics, and pursue full compensation for your medical expenses, lost income, and long-term recovery needs. Our Bellevue truck accident lawyers have the experience and resources to hold negligent drivers and trucking companies accountable. Contact us today for a free consultation and let us help you move forward with confidence.

#TruckAccidentLawyer #BellevueNE #PersonalInjuryLaw #HauptmanOBrien

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/truck-accidents-in-bellevue-why-these-cases-require-experienced-legal-help/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #01: Publish between 09/08 - 09/12 (if possible), on the Council Bluffs, IA GBP:
607 S Main St, Council Bluffs, IA 51503, United States

Iowa’s new hands-free driving law, effective July 1, 2025, makes it illegal to hold a phone while behind the wheel. While this law is designed to reduce distracted driving and save lives, it also plays a key role in car accident claims. If another driver was using their phone and caused your crash, proving liability may now be easier. Police reports, phone records, and eyewitness accounts can strengthen your case, and insurance companies may be more inclined to settle fairly when the law is clearly violated.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, LLC, our experienced car accident attorneys know how to investigate distracted driving crashes and fight for the compensation you deserve. Don’t face rising medical bills and lost wages alone. Schedule your free case review today and let us protect your rights.

#DistractedDriving #IowaLaw #CarAccidentLawyer #HandsFreeDriving

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/iowas-hands-free-driving-law/


POSTED 9/11 - COUNCIL BLUFFS - https://share.google/rQXcWWEwaebuMclp2
====================================================

POST #2: Publish between 09/08 - 09/12 (if possible), on the Bellevue, NE GBP:
11507 S 42nd St # 101, Bellevue, NE 68123, United States

Receiving a settlement offer after a personal injury can feel like a relief, but accepting too soon may leave you with regrets. Insurance companies often push quick, lowball offers designed to protect their bottom line, not your future. Before agreeing to settle, it’s crucial to consider the seriousness of your injuries, whether you’ve reached Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI), the strength of your case, and the statute of limitations. Settling too early can mean missing out on compensation for future medical care, lost wages, pain and suffering, or reduced quality of life.

Our attorneys at Hauptman, O’Brien Personal Injury Lawyers fight to ensure our clients don’t settle for less than they deserve. With nearly 300 years of combined experience, we provide the guidance needed to protect your rights and secure fair compensation. Schedule a free consultation today and let us safeguard your future.

#PersonalInjuryLawyer #FairCompensation #LegalAdvice #InjuryClaim

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/factors-to-consider-before-settling-a-personal-injury-claim/


POSTED 9/15 - BELLEVUE - https://share.google/AJVuMsEtxfelfFgMT
====================================================

POST #03: Publish between 09/15 - 09/19 (if possible), on the Council Bluffs, IA GBP: 607 S Main St, Council Bluffs, IA 51503, United States.

Iowa recently updated its fireworks law to improve safety, but accidents still happen, and the results can be devastating. Firework injuries often involve burns, eye damage, hearing loss, or even loss of limbs. When an accident occurs, determining liability isn’t always straightforward. Property owners, event organizers, manufacturers, or even marketers could be held responsible if negligence played a role. The process of proving fault and negotiating fair compensation can be overwhelming, especially while dealing with medical bills and lost wages.

That’s why the personal injury attorneys at Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, LLC are here to help. We investigate thoroughly, handle negotiations, and are ready to take your case to court if necessary. Don’t navigate this alone. Contact our team today for a free consultation and protect your right to compensation.

#FireworksSafety #IowaLaw #PersonalInjury #LegalHelp

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/iowas-2025-fireworks-law/

Note: This website blog post is scheduled to go live on 09/13/2025.

====================================================

POST #04: Publish between 09/19 - 09/26 (if possible) on the main Omaha, NE GBP:
1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States

Delivery trucks from Amazon, FedEx, and UPS are everywhere on our roads, and accidents involving these vehicles can cause devastating injuries. With large corporate legal teams working to minimize responsibility, victims often face an uphill battle. Liability can be complex, especially when companies use contractors, skip maintenance, or pressure drivers to meet unrealistic deadlines. Injuries from these crashes can include brain trauma, spinal cord damage, or even loss of limbs.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, LLC, we understand how these companies operate and know how to fight back. From gathering driver logs and maintenance records to negotiating with insurers, our attorneys are committed to protecting your rights and securing the compensation you deserve. Don’t face corporate giants alone. Schedule your free consultation today and let us stand by your side.

#TruckAccidentLawyer #AmazonAccident #FedExUPSAccidents #PersonalInjury

Learn More:
https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/amazon-fedex-ups-truck-accident-liability/

Note: This website blog post is scheduled to go live on 09/17/2025

====================================================

POST #05: Publish between 09/22 - 09/30 (if possible) on the South Omaha GBP:
4939 S 24th St, Omaha, NE 68107, United States

When a truck accident happens, the negligent driver may not be the only one responsible. Under vicarious liability, trucking companies can also be held accountable for the actions of their employees. This means victims may have access to greater compensation through company insurance policies, helping cover medical bills, lost wages, and long-term care needs. Liability can also extend to leasing agencies, manufacturers, mechanics, freight brokers, or cargo loaders. Proving fault in these cases requires experience, resources, and an understanding of federal trucking regulations.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, LLC, our truck accident lawyers investigate every angle to identify all liable parties and fight for maximum recovery. Don’t face powerful corporations and insurers alone—our team is ready to protect your rights and help you move forward after a devastating accident. Contact us today for a free case review.

#TruckAccidentLawyer #VicariousLiability #PersonalInjury #JusticeForVictims

title: Locality Pages across 1-80 Corridor
description:
Task: Create Locality pages for areas near the I-80 corridor. 

In December's reporting call Corey, Tim, and Dave discussed expanding HOWL into other local towns/cities near the I-80 corridors without a physical location and GBP. 

The client was asked to follow up with the towns that would be relevant versus us picking and choosing and they got that back to us right after today's reporting call. Here they are: 

Lincoln, Columbus, Norfolk, Grand Island, Beatrice, Kearney, Auburn, North Platte, Hastings, Fremont,
Gothenburg, Ogallala, Scottsbluff, Valentine, McCook, O’Neill, Holdrege, Cozad, Broken Bow, Chadron

Can you let us know:
  • how many of these locality pages can we create within our budget per month
  • If we'll need to give the client a heads up on budget increase or one-time invoice as they get created. 
  • When this work can start and how many we'll start with and the turnaround time. 
Thanks!

title: Add GBP Posts
description:
Understanding Personal Injury Depositions: Why Preparation Matters

If you're pursuing a personal injury claim, one of the most important steps in the process is your deposition. While it may seem intimidating, a well-prepared deposition can significantly impact the outcome of your case. At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, we understand that this part of the legal process can be stressful—which is why we’re here to help you prepare every step of the way.

A deposition is your opportunity to tell your story under oath. It's not held in a courtroom but is still a serious, recorded event. You’ll answer questions about how your injury occurred, the medical care you’ve received, and how the accident has affected your life. Your testimony becomes part of the case record and can play a crucial role in settlement negotiations or a court trial.

Preparation is key. We guide you through reviewing your accident details, clarifying your injuries, and understanding the legal arguments in your case. We also help you avoid common mistakes, like guessing answers or becoming emotional under pressure. With our support, you’ll feel confident, composed, and ready.

Need help with your personal injury case? Contact Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop today for a free consultation.

#PersonalInjuryLaw #DepositionTips #LegalSupport #HauptmanOBrien

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/what-to-expect-during-your-personal-injury-deposition-process/

1005 S 107th Ave Suite 200, Omaha, NE 68114, United States



===============================================
What to Expect During a Personal Injury Deposition – And Why It Matters

If you’ve filed a personal injury claim, chances are you’ll need to go through a deposition. For many, this is one of the most nerve-wracking parts of the process—but it doesn’t have to be. At Hauptman O’Brien, we make sure our clients are fully informed, prepared, and supported every step of the way.

Think of your deposition as your chance to clearly explain your side of the story. You’ll be asked questions by the opposing attorney about the incident, your injuries, your treatment, and how the accident has impacted your life. Everything you say is recorded and can later be used in court.

That’s why preparation is so important. We help you understand what questions to expect, how to avoid common pitfalls, and how to speak with confidence and clarity. From avoiding emotional reactions to staying consistent in your answers, your deposition can strengthen your case—and sometimes even lead to a favorable settlement before trial.

Your story matters. Make sure it’s told right. Contact Hauptman O’Brien to get the guidance and advocacy you deserve.

#InjuryLaw #KnowYourRights #LegalAdvice #HauptmanOBrien

https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/what-to-expect-during-your-personal-injury-deposition-process/

4939 S 24th St, Omaha, NE 68107, United States

title: Add GBP Post
description:
🚴‍♀️ Cycling is becoming a popular choice for commuting, fitness, and fun across Nebraska—but it also comes with increased risks. Bicycle accidents involving motor vehicles can lead to serious injuries and significant financial burdens. In many cases, auto insurance may cover your damages, but navigating the claims process isn’t always straightforward.

At Hauptman O’Brien, we understand the complexities of Nebraska’s insurance laws and are dedicated to helping injured cyclists recover the compensation they deserve. Whether it’s negotiating with insurance companies or taking your case to court, our experienced attorneys are here to support you every step of the way.

If you or someone you love has been injured in a bicycle accident, reach out today for a free consultation. Let us help you focus on healing while we handle the rest.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
When a workplace injury leads to a tragic loss of life, Nebraska’s workers’ compensation laws provide financial support to surviving family members. Under state law, a surviving spouse is entitled to two-thirds of the deceased worker’s wages until they remarry. Additionally, dependent children can receive financial benefits, and up to $6,000 is available for funeral expenses.

However, if negligence played a role in the accident, families may also pursue a wrongful death claim against the employer or a third party. At Hauptman O'Brien, our South Omaha personal injury attorneys help families navigate these complex cases to seek justice and full compensation for their loss. If you have lost a loved one in a workplace accident, contact us today to discuss your legal options.

#WorkersComp #WrongfulDeath #NebraskaLawyer #WorkplaceAccidents

This data can help determine whether the truck driver or trucking company was negligent.

At Hauptman O'Brien, our experienced truck accident attorneys in Bellevue, NE, know how to access and analyze black box data to strengthen your claim. description:
After an accident, social media can become your biggest enemy. Insurance adjusters and defense attorneys will search for anything online to discredit your claim. A simple post, check-in, or even a friend's tag can be twisted to suggest your injuries aren’t as serious as you claim.

Even with strict privacy settings, your posts may still be used against you. To protect your case, avoid sharing updates about your accident, medical condition, or activities. Disable tagging, turn off location tracking, and never accept new friend requests.

The safest approach? Stay off social media entirely until your case is resolved. If you’ve been injured and need legal guidance, our experienced attorneys can help ensure your rights are protected. Contact us today for a free consultation.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Who Is Covered Under Iowa Workers’ Compensation Laws?

If you’ve been injured on the job, you may be entitled to workers’ compensation benefits. In Iowa, nearly all employees—full-time, part-time, seasonal, and some temporary workers—are covered from day one. Employers with at least one employee must provide workers’ comp. However, independent contractors, volunteers, and certain agricultural and family business workers may be exempt.

Workers’ comp covers injuries from accidents, repetitive strain, and occupational illnesses. However, injuries from intentional acts, substance abuse, or off-duty incidents may not be covered.

Understanding your rights is crucial. If you’ve been injured at work, our attorneys at Hauptman O'Brien, PC can help ensure you receive the benefits you deserve. Contact us today for a free consultation.


#WorkersCompensation #IowaLaw #InjuredAtWork #LegalHelp #HauptmanOBrien #FreeConsultation

description:
Receiving a denial for your car insurance claim can be frustrating, but it doesn’t have to be the end of the road. Understanding why your claim was denied is the first step toward challenging the decision. Common reasons include policy exclusions, insufficient documentation, or disputes over fault.

Reviewing your insurance policy, gathering additional evidence, and filing an appeal can improve your chances of reversing the denial. If your insurer refuses to cooperate, you can file a complaint with state regulators or explore legal action.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, P.C., we help car accident victims fight unfair claim denials. Contact us today for a free consultation.

#CarAccidentClaims #InsuranceDenial #KnowYourRights #PersonalInjuryLawyer

Related task: Re: GBP posts - J296 Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf, & Lathrop

After an Iowa car accident, it’s crucial to notify your insurance company as soon as possible. However, this step requires caution—what you say can impact your claim.

Insurance adjusters aim to minimize payouts, so avoid giving unnecessary details about your injuries or admitting fault. Instead, stick to the basics: the date, time, location, and parties involved. If possible, let a personal injury lawyer handle these communications for you. An attorney knows how to navigate insurers' tactics and protect your rights.

Need help dealing with the insurance company? Our experienced motor vehicle accident attorneys are here to support you. 📞 Call us today for a free consultation.

Did you know expert witnesses can play a crucial role in car accident cases? Unlike eyewitnesses, who describe what they saw, expert witnesses help clarify complex technical, medical, and financial details.

Accident reconstructionists can determine how a crash happened, medical experts can explain the severity of injuries, and economists can assess financial damages. Even if your case doesn’t go to trial, expert testimony can add credibility and increase your chances of a favorable settlement.

At Hauptman, O’Brien, P.C., we work with skilled experts who can help strengthen your claim. If you’ve been in a car accident, contact us today for a free consultation—you don’t pay unless we win.

title: Get Med- Mal Page Form Info from Shelby for Dev
description:
• Fed follow up w/ Shelby to get this project moving forward. 
  • Notify dev team when it's ready for review for a timeline.


    , can you review Shelby's request. I want to make sure this is something we can provide or if we need may need to consider invoicing: 

    We are looking to streamline the process for handling inquiries related to medical malpractice by directing callers to a dedicated page on our website. This page will feature a brief introduction to medical malpractice and will include a form for individuals to submit their inquiries.
 
In researching potential models for this page, I came across a firm in Florida that has a form very similar to what we are aiming to implement. On our page, however, we would like to see an additional area where guests have the option to upload medical records or death certificates. I am wondering if there is a way for your team to create a mock page using our preamble and a form like the one used by the other law firm. This would allow us to gather input from all the attorneys and make any necessary changes or additions before finalizing the page. We have not yet determined where submitted questionnaires will be directed.
 
Link: Medical Malpractice Intake Questionnaire - Florin|Roebig
 
Preamble:
 
Thank you for contacting Hauptman O’Brien concerning your medical malpractice claim. Due to the complex nature of medical malpractice matters, we have found that the best way to evaluate these cases is to have you complete the below questionnaire and provide a summary of you or your loved one’s claim. Additionally, if you have access to the medical records or death certificate, we kindly request that you submit them along with the completed questionnaire.
 
We will make every effort to review and respond to your request within five business days. Please be advised that accepting your submission does not imply representation in your matter. However, we assure you that your information will remain confidential and will not be shared with any other party.
 
Prior to your submission, it is crucial to note that in the state of Nebraska and Iowa (the only two states our firm is licensed to practice law in) there is a two-year statute of limitations for medical malpractice claims. This means that any claim must be resolved or filed within two years of the alleged malpractice, or the individual will be forever barred from bringing a claim. While there are exceptions to the statute, such as cases involving minors, continuous treatment, or discovery of the malpractice after the two-year period, it is important to be mindful of these time limitations. If your potential claim is approaching the two-year statute of limitations, we cannot guarantee protection from the statute in the event that it is about to expire and prior to any formal representation agreement between the parties is commenced.
 
Before you begin the questionnaire, it’s important to understand the Plaintiff’s Burden of Proof in a medical malpractice claim. As the claimant, you must be able to prove the following:
 
  1. An error committed by a nurse, doctor, or other medical professional, indicating that more than just a bad outcome occurred. We must establish that a specific error was committed, breaching the standard of care.
  2. A casual connection between the error and an injury. It is essential to define how the medical error directly led to a specific injury.
  3. The injury resulted in damages such as medical expenses, pain and suffering, lost income, etc.
 
 
Please let me know if you have any questions.

description:
In Nebraska, pedestrian accidents often involve intricate issues of liability and legal rights. Drivers are required to exercise reasonable care to prevent hitting pedestrians, and their failure to do so can lead to liability for any resulting injuries. Pedestrians also have legal duties, such as adhering to traffic signals and using designated crosswalks. Determining liability may involve evaluating the actions of both the driver and the pedestrian at the time of the accident. At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, our personal injury attorneys offer expert legal support to ensure that the rights of pedestrians are upheld. We assist victims in navigating the legal system, helping them secure the compensation they need for medical expenses, lost wages, and other damages. Learn more about your legal rights as a pedestrian, here: https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/liability-and-legal-rights-in-nebraska-pedestrian-accidents/

#PersonalInjuryAttorney #pedestrianaccidents #OmahaNE #HauptmanObrienWolfLathrop #FreeConsultation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Nebraska imposes strict liability on dog bite cases. Therefore, under state law, a dog’s owner can be held responsible for injuries and damages regardless of whether or not the dog has a history of aggressive behavior. Other states follow some version of the “one-bite” rule, which is based on the legal theory of negligence. Typically, under this law, pet owners can only be held liable for injuries their dog caused if they knew the animal had a history of being aggressive or a propensity towards violence. If you or a loved one is pursuing compensation for a dog bite, it’s in your best interests to contact an attorney sooner rather than later. Our animal bite injury attorneys will tell you what to do. Learn more: https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/liability-in-dog-bite-cases/

If you’ve received a personal injury settlement, you may wonder about its tax implications. Understanding which parts of your settlement are taxable can help you plan effectively. However, other components like lost wages, punitive damages, and interest on settlements may be taxable.

Navigating these complexities can be overwhelming, but you don’t have to do it alone. An experienced personal injury attorney can guide you through the process, ensuring you understand how your settlement is categorized and helping you secure the compensation you deserve.

This law encourages individuals to provide voluntary assistance during emergencies without worrying about civil liability. Whether you’re offering CPR, helping at a car accident scene, or assisting during a medical crisis, you are protected as long as you act in good faith and without expecting compensation. The law also covers emergencies involving drug overdoses or alcohol-related incidents, allowing people to act quickly without hesitation.

description:
Were you involved in a car accident but weren’t wearing your seat belt? You may still be entitled to compensation for your injuries. In Nebraska, while seat belts are required, not wearing one doesn’t automatically bar you from recovering damages. Nebraska follows a comparative negligence system, meaning your compensation may be reduced based on your percentage of fault, but as long as you are less than 50% at fault, you can still pursue damages. A skilled personal injury attorney can help build a strong case, focusing on the other party's negligence and working to minimize the impact of the seat belt defense. Your attorney will gather evidence, negotiate with insurance companies, and ensure you receive the maximum compensation for your injuries. Contact Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf, & Lathrop today for a free case evaluation. Our team is dedicated to protecting your rights and helping you recover after a serious car accident.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
To obtain compensation for medical expenses, property damage, lost wages, and other losses from the other party's insurance company after an accident, you might need to submit a demand letter.

A demand letter is a formal request sent to the insurance company, detailing the incident, injuries sustained, and financial losses suffered. It allows you to present your claim clearly and concisely, setting the stage for settlement negotiations.

Here are the main steps for writing a vehicle accident demand letter:

Step 1: Organize Necessary Information
Step 2: Provide Basic Information
Step 3: Describe the Auto Accident
Step 4: Outline Your Injuries and Medical Treatment
Step 5: Explain the Impact on Your Life
Step 6: List Your Financial Damages
Step 7: State Your Total Demand for Compensation
Step 8: Conclude the Demand Letter

Learn more on how to tackle each one of the steps above, here: https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/how-to-write-an-auto-accident-demand-letter/

title: Change all website forms submission email addresses to intake@omahapersonalinjury.com
CWS Safe Ride Home(R)...
description:
Hi Keith,

This is inline w/ the ticket forwarded today. I have copied and pasted the request below for complete info:

--Email from the client--
Good afternoon!
 
As you know, our firm does a Safe Ride Home® program through Uber for numerous events in our area. One of our largest campaigns is for Omaha’s College World Series which is approaching very quickly. Opening day is Friday, June 14th and it runs through Monday, June 24th. With that being said, we will need some temporary changes made on our Safe Ride Home page. We would like to utilize the same form from St. Patrick’s Day where visitors input their name, phone number, and email address. As you likely remember, the Uber voucher is then automatically sent to their email. Both the email & voucher redemption guide are attached. Additional information:

  • Uber Voucher Link: https://r.uber.com/rBPYs3JKAQJ
  • The temporary CWS banner to replace the current photo is attached.
  • Voucher is worth $35
  • Yellow Banner:
    • Current: “No current Safe Ride Home® events. Please check this page periodically as we are always adding new events.
    • Change to: “Hey College World Series fans! Play it safe. Claim your Safe Ride Home® now!”

  • Fine Print to be included below campaign information: **To redeem discount, Uber Voucher code rBPYs3JKAQJ must be applied to Payment section of the Uber App prior to requesting the intended trip. Uber Voucher valid June 14, 2024, 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 15, 2024, 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 16, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 17, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 18, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 19, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 20, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 21, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 22, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, June 23, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM, & 6:00 PM-11:59 PM, & June 25, 2024 12:00 AM-2:00 AM. Uber Voucher expires June 25, 2024, 02:00 AM. Uber Voucher is only valid for rides requested placed using applicable Uber App. Taxes and other fees will be covered provided that the value of the Uber Voucher is greater than the total order amount. Offer is non-transferable, subject to change or cancellation. Issues involving redemption and/or use of the Uber Voucher code should be directed to Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop at attorneys@hauptman-obrien.net. Driver tips are not included.
 
If possible, these temporary changes should be completed and on the website on June 4th as we will push paid advertising across our socials at that time. On the morning of Tuesday, June 25th, the page should be restored to current settings. I did notice, however, that there is a necessary change needed on our current page. The circled area should be removed if there is not an active campaign. Please also remove “Please see below for the dates that we will be offering safe rides home” but keep “Please check this page periodically as we are always adding new events”.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Determining fault in car accidents is crucial, impacting insurance claims and legal outcomes. Understanding who is responsible can be straightforward or complex, depending on the circumstances. It involves examining police reports, collecting evidence, and analyzing witness testimonies. Nebraska follows a comparative negligence system, where fault is distributed based on each party's degree of responsibility. Insurance companies conduct their own investigations and may assign fault to multiple parties. Disputing an insurer’s decision is possible through negotiations or additional evidence. Learn more: https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/determining-fault-in-bellevue-car-accidents/

#CarAccidents #personalinjuryattorneys #BellevueNE #freeconsultation
This is for the Bellevue, NE GBP: 
11507 South 42nd Street
Suite 101
Bellevue, NE 68123

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Negotiating with insurance companies after an accident can be challenging. These profit-driven businesses prioritize their financial interests, often offering low settlements or outright denying valid claims. Learn more here: https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/blog/negotiating-with-insurers/

At Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop, our skilled Bellevue personal injury attorneys understand the tactics insurers use and are prepared to counter them effectively. We gather comprehensive evidence, negotiate persistently, and ensure you understand the true value of your claim. Don’t let insurance companies take advantage of you. Contact us today for a free case review and let our experienced negotiators fight for the compensation you deserve.

title: St. Patrick's Day SRH
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Sky Ortigas
description:
We will be offering our Safe Ride Home® Program for St. Patrick’s Day weekend & would like our website updated as soon as possible so we can begin advertising & people can start claiming their rides. In the past, we added a form to the https://www.hauptman-obrien.net/safe-ride-home/ page. This form requires the visitor to enter their name, phone number, and email address. From there, the voucher is automatically sent to their email. I have attached an example of an email riders received in the past. Please update the ride amount to $35 and change out the code. I attached the Voucher Redemption Guide. We need these email addresses provided to us on Monday, March 18th so we may begin sending out Safe Ride Home reviews.
 
Please update the current photo on the site to the attached photo. Please also update the yellow banner below that photo to say something along the lines of “Don’t miss out! Claim your Safe Ride Home® for St. Patrick’s Day.”
 
Information for Clixsy:
Link: https://r.uber.com/r4imABhxi80
Code: r4imABhxi80
Fine Print: **To redeem discount, Uber Voucher code r4imABhxi80 must be applied to Payment section of the Uber App prior to requesting the intended trip. Uber Voucher valid March 16, 2024,12:00 PM, through March 18, 2024,12:00 AM. Uber Voucher expires March 18, 2024,12:00 AM. Uber Voucher is only valid for rides requested placed using applicable Uber App. Taxes and other fees will be covered provided that the value of the Uber Voucher is greater than the total order amount. Offer is non-transferable, subject to change or cancellation. Issues involving redemption and/or use of the Uber Voucher code should be directed to Hauptman, O’Brien, Wolf & Lathrop at attorneys@hauptman-obrien.net. Driver tips are not included.
 

 
  • The yellow banner needs to say: Don’t miss out! Claim your Safe Ride Home® for St. Patrick’s Day.
  • Yes, the email needs to be sent to every individual who opts into the form. I attached the email in a word document.

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466

description:
DISCOVERY MEETING INTERNAL CHECKLIST


Have you discussed the Client's Business/Campaign Goals? One goal per campaign.

Have you built a Typical Customer Journey for the Client's business?

Have you built a Typical Buyer Persona for each campaign?

Have you decided on Campaign KPIs to measure our success?

Have you agreed on Monthly KPI Targets?

Have you set Campaign Budgets?

Have you spoken about common objections to sales the Client has experienced?

Have you done a SWOT analysis on each of the Client's Top 3 competitors?

Has Client intimated any legal constraints to advertising?

Have you discussed Integrated Marketing and looked at Client's past and current advertising campaigns?

Has the Client brought images/logos etc.?

Have you brainstormed ideas for creatives/messages/slogans/copy etc.?

Have you discussed incentives/discounts for referrals?

Have you asked for referrals? Add the on the next sheet.
